macro_context.market_sentiment: Overall crypto market mood is cautious to fearful. BTC is testing the $75K support level with elevated volatility; altcoins are seeing steeper drawdowns than Bitcoin, suggesting a flight-to-safety rotation into BTC. The market rose modestly on April 19-20 after several days of fear-driven selling.
macro_context.btc_dominance: BTC dominance is approximately 57-59%, near multi-month highs, signaling capital rotating into Bitcoin as the perceived safe haven amid broader crypto weakness. Rising dominance trend confirms BTC outperforming altcoins.
macro_context.total_market_cap: Total crypto market cap is approximately $2.5-2.7 trillion as of mid-to-late April 2026, down from a KuCoin-cited peak of ~$3.5T earlier in 2026. Recent 24-hour change showed a modest +2.8% recovery but the multi-week trend is negative.
macro_context.fear_greed_index: Fear & Greed Index is reading approximately 27-29 (Fear). This is the lowest reading in three weeks. In early April the index briefly touched 23 (Extreme Fear) while retail sentiment remained deeply negative even as institutional buyers were accumulating.
macro_context.fed_outlook: The Fed's current restrictive stance continues to weigh on risk assets including crypto. Macro monetary tightening is suppressing speculative capital flows; however, any pivot signals could be a significant tailwind for Bitcoin. Current environment keeps crypto correlated to equities on the downside.
macro_context.institutional_flows: Mixed but net positive on a monthly basis. Spot BTC ETFs recorded ~$325M net outflows on April 13 led by Fidelity FBTC (-$229M) and ARK ARKB (-$63M), but BlackRock IBIT pulled in $284M in a single day on April 17. US spot BTC ETFs have logged $921M in net inflows over a recent 5-day window, the largest monthly whale accumulation since 2013. Strategy (MicroStrategy) added 13,927 BTC (~$1B) on April 13, bringing total treasury to ~780,000+ BTC. Strategy is now neck-and-neck with BlackRock IBIT (~796K BTC) for the title of largest public Bitcoin holder.
macro_context.regulatory: The SEC has issued guidance treating select stablecoins as near-cash (2% haircut for broker-dealers), signaling stablecoin institutionalization. SEC Chair Paul Atkins stated 'Regulation Crypto' is at the final OIRA review stage before publication. The CLARITY Act compromise on stablecoin yields is advancing toward a Senate Banking Committee markup in late April. The Trump administration has not yet released the formal Strategic Bitcoin Reserve design but is expected to do so ahead of a July 22, 2026 deadline. Overall regulatory trend is moving toward clarity and legitimacy, which is broadly supportive for Bitcoin.
key_events: Senate Banking Committee CLARITY Act / stablecoin yield markup expected late April 2026
SEC 'Regulation Crypto' proposal publication imminent (post-OIRA clearance)
U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve design due by July 22, 2026 deadline
BlackRock Q1 2026 earnings with IBIT AUM at $54B — institutional sentiment gauge
Bitcoin Core v31.0 recently released with cluster mempool and privacy upgrades (Tor/I2P default)
Deutsche Boerse acquired $200M stake in Kraken — major institutional crypto infrastructure signal

ticker_news.headline: Strategy (MicroStrategy) adds 13,927 BTC at avg $71,902, bringing total treasury to ~781K BTC
Bitcoin Core v31.0 released — cluster mempool redesign improves fee estimation, Tor/I2P default broadcasting enhances privacy
Bitcoin tests $75K support as Fear & Greed drops to 27; altcoins lead losses with Solana down 3%+
US spot BTC ETFs log $921M net inflows over 5-day window; BlackRock IBIT pulls $284M single-day on April 17
Deutsche Boerse acquires $200M stake in Kraken — watershed moment for institutional crypto infrastructure acceptance

on_chain_signals.whale_activity: Strongly accumulative over the past 30 days. Whale addresses (1,000+ BTC) grew from 2,082 to 2,140 wallets (+58), collectively accumulating 270,000 BTC — the largest monthly whale accumulation since 2013. However, on April 15, hourly exchange inflows spiked to 11,000 BTC (highest since Dec 2025), a large-holder-driven inflow near the $75K resistance level, which historically precedes short-term corrections. Net 48,200 BTC left exchanges over the prior 30 days; exchange reserves at 2.21M BTC (5.88% of supply) — the lowest since December 2017. Mixed short-term signal (inflow spike at resistance) but medium-term trend is accumulation/withdrawal.
on_chain_signals.funding_rates: Live data not directly retrieved from CoinGlass; based on market context, funding rates are likely near-neutral to slightly negative given fear sentiment and the recent sell-off from highs above $77K. Negative or low funding rates suggest the market is not overleveraged to the long side, which reduces downside liquidation risk and can be a constructive setup for a recovery. Sources: CoinGlass, CryptoQuant (live tracking recommended).
on_chain_signals.exchange_flows: Net outflows dominate the 30-day trend: monthly average exchange netflow has been -1,640 BTC/day since March 1, indicating sustained accumulation off-exchange. The April 15 inflow spike (11,000 BTC/hour at $75K resistance) is a cautionary short-term signal suggesting some large holders may be distributing into resistance. Bitcoin exchange reserves hit a 9-year low (5.88% of supply), which structurally reduces available sell-side supply.

development_activity: Bitcoin Core v31.0 was released in April 2026, delivering a cluster mempool redesign for improved fee estimation accuracy and default Tor/I2P transaction broadcasting for enhanced privacy. BIPs repository remains active. Broader crypto developer activity has declined ~75% in weekly commits and ~56% in active developers since early 2025, largely due to talent migration to AI projects. Bitcoin core protocol development remains steady relative to altcoins. Sources: github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin, coindesk.com (March 12 2026), intellectia.ai.
upcoming_catalysts: Near-term: (1) CLARITY Act stablecoin markup in Senate Banking Committee (late April) — passage would be a positive regulatory milestone; (2) SEC 'Regulation Crypto' formal proposal publication — brings certainty to crypto asset classification; (3) U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve design release due by July 22, 2026 — any active-buying language would be a strong positive catalyst; (4) Continued ETF inflow momentum, especially if macro conditions stabilize (S&P 500 recently hit record high, potentially unlocking risk appetite). Longer-term: no Bitcoin halving imminent (next halving ~2028). Bitcoin Core upgrade roadmap continues incrementally. No major hard forks expected. Key risk event: further macro deterioration or Fed hawkishness could reverse ETF inflows quickly.
